channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kolkata Man Dies in Hospital Lift After Being Trapped for Over an Hour
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded at the R.G. Kar Medical College and Hospital in Kolkata where a 40‑year‑old man, identified as Arup Bandopadhyay, lost his life after being trapped inside a malfunctioning e...

item.title: Burundian Refugees Return Home Voluntarily Ahead of Nyarugusu and Nduta Camp Closures
item.description: Thousands of Burundian refugees living in Nduta camp in Kibondo District and Nyarugusu camp in Kasulu District, Kigoma Region, Tanzania, have continued returning to their home country, Burundi, on vol...

item.title: Manchester United and Bournemouth Play Out 2-2 Draw in EPL Thriller
item.description: For Friday night EPL match, Manchester United and Bournemouth knack waka enter better drama as dem share points 2-2. Bruno Fernandes shine gobe, score penalty plus small help with own goal wey put Man...

item.title: DOH plans to buy nearly 7 million flu vaccines for priority groups
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) announced plans to procure approximately 6.9 million doses of influenza vaccines for 2026, aiming to protect vulnerable populations such as senior citizens and health wo...

item.title: Alex Mwakideu Shares His Views on Gender Roles in Marriage
item.description: Radio presenter and YouTuber Alex Mwakideu recently shared his perspective on marriage and household responsibilities, sparking discussion online. He stated that once a man is married, he should not e...

item.title: Poll shows One Nation gaining strong ground in South Australian politics
item.description: A recent poll in South Australia suggests that One Nation is making striking gains in voter support, potentially signalling a broader cultural and political shift in the state. Conducted by research f...

item.title: Community solar lighting brings safety and hope to inner-city Johannesburg
item.description: A growing solar lighting initiative is making inner-city Johannesburg safer, especially for women who used to fear walking after dark. Since July 2024, more than 2,000 solar-powered lights have been i...

item.title: Iain Njiraini Calls for Political Decency as Ruto and Gachagua Trade Personal Insults
item.description: Political commentator and commercial aviator Iain Njiraini has spoken out strongly about the recent public exchanges between President William Ruto and his former deputy, Rigathi Gachagua. The two lea...

item.title: Karnataka Government Unveils Comprehensive Plan to Ease Bengaluru’s Traffic Congestion
item.description: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar has introduced a new urban mobility policy aimed at addressing Bengaluru’s chronic traffic congestion, one of the city’s most persistent civic challenge...

item.title: Heavy Rain Triggers Mudslide in Coquitlam and Evacuation Alerts in Fraser Valley
item.description: A prolonged atmospheric river has brought intense rainfall across British Columbia, causing significant hazards in several regions. In Coquitlam, a mudslide on Pipeline Road near the Coquitlam River f...

item.title: Cuba Restricts Diesel Supply to U.S. Embassy Generators
item.description: The United States Embassy in Havana, Cuba, is facing a significant challenge as the Cuban government has refused a request to import diesel fuel needed to operate its generators. This restriction come...

item.title: Filipino Muslims observe Eid'l Fitr with prayers and family gatherings
item.description: Filipino Muslims across the country gathered on March 21, 2026, to celebrate Eid'l Fitr, marking the end of the holy month of Ramadan. In Manila, thousands attended early morning prayers at Quirino Gr...

item.title: Dame Sarah Mullally to Become First Female Archbishop of Canterbury
item.description: Dame Sarah Mullally will make history as the first female Archbishop of Canterbury, taking on the Church of England's highest clerical office. Her official enthronement is set to take place at Canterb...

item.title: Aristote Kagombe: From Hair Stylist to Real Estate Investor in Tanzania
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, Tanzanian stylist Aristote Kagombe has become a symbol of determination and creativity in the beauty industry. Starting with humble beginnings, he entered women’s salons without form...

item.title: UK Authorises US Strikes on Iran Missile Sites from British Bases
item.description: The United Kingdom has, after some initial hesitation, granted permission for the United States to utilise military bases within the country to launch strikes against Iranian missile sites. These site...

item.title: Trump Signals Potential Drawdown in Iran Conflict Amid Mixed Messages
item.description: President Trump may be signaling a potential winding down of the U.S. military involvement in Iran, though his statements remain inconsistent. On the White House south lawn, Trump claimed that the U.S...

item.title: Neighbours and Well-Wishers Rescue Young Boy from Alleged Abuse in Githurai
item.description: A six-year-old boy from Githurai 44 has been rescued after reports of alleged abuse by his father and stepmother emerged. A concerned neighbour contacted philanthropist Ndundu Nyoro, explaining that t...

item.title: Government and Partners Unite to Restore Oloolua Forest for Sustainable Development
item.description: The Oloolua Forest Restoration Initiative in Nairobi County has received a major boost after a high-level fundraising dinner brought together senior government officials, development partners, and key...

item.title: Lagos short-let sector records N281bn revenue in 2025 despite regulations and competition
item.description: The Lagos short-let market generate around N281.03 billion in 2025, according to Edala Development report. Even with inflation and more players entering the business, the market still hold strong. Lek...

item.title: KUCCPS Launches Automated Placement System to Improve University Admission Process
item.description: Kenya University and Colleges Central Placement Service (KUCCPS) is set to implement a new automated placement system starting from the 2026/2027 academic cycle. The revamped portal will automatically...
